text:
There was an old cat with a penchant for hats. Everywhere he went, heads would turn. One particularly sunny afternoon, he sat patiently atop the grand oak tree. Onlookers peeked over their noses. Then...

With remarkable elegance for its size, the cat tipped his hat wayyyy backward. Laughter erupted.

As evening settled in, the cat lazily descended. Heads still aplenty. He plopped onto a young girl's lap.

She giggled.

"You wear that thing all day? That's so clever!"

"I just think it looks awesome," she confessed. And that night, she slept snugly stuffed inside an old hat.

That hat had many lives...

It'd wander off to school... the boy cherished it. They fought fair. One day...

"I'll show you next time!"

But next time was never told.

The hat lived another day.

It found its way into a children's book. An owl loved it.

That owl wrote a letter: "Give Owl
